meaning: n.生物地球化学(研究一地区内生物物质及其与地球化学物质的关系)
examples: The advances in marine biogeochemistry studies in China since 1998 are reviewed in this paper.
In addition, carbon and nitrogen isotope and its ratio was important indicator in the riverine biogeochemistry study.
Anaerobic methane oxidation (AMO) is a globally important biogeochemistry process, which has been identified by sufficient geochemical evidence.
